blocks.content: Daisy went with him. She just booked her flight when he booked his, and he didn't say anything.
Ruth's house was the same as it had always been. She met them at the door in jeans and a cardigan and looked thinner than the summer.
They sat in her kitchen for three hours that first night. She talked about the treatment — what it involved, what the doctors had said about timeline.
Daisy did the dishes. It was something to do with her hands.
At some point, past ten, Ruth reached across the table and covered Kit's hand with hers.

blocks.speaker: Kit's Mom
blocks.content: I need you to hear me. I have had a good life. I have had you. I have had your film, and your wedding, and that girl doing my dishes right now — I have had all of that. Whatever comes next, I am not afraid of it.
She patted his hand and got up to check on the stove. Kit sat at his mother's kitchen table.
He put his face in his hands.
She dried her hands on the dish towel. Crossed the kitchen. Put her hand on his back.
Ruth came back to the table and did the same on the other side. Mother and wife, on either side of him.
